skill.name: obsidian-vault
skill.description: Search, create, and organize notes in a local Obsidian vault using filename searches, content searches, wikilinks, and index notes.
skill.summary: Manage a local Obsidian vault with search, note creation, wikilinks, backlinks, and index notes.
skill.author: mattpocock

security_audit.summary: The weak cryptography static finding at SKILL.md:3 is a false positive; that line is only the skill description. The external command findings are mostly Markdown examples, but the skill does instruct agents to run find and grep against a local Obsidian vault. No evidence found of prompt injection, network access, credential access, or malicious intent.

medium_findings.title: Local Vault Access Through Shell Commands
medium_findings.description: The skill gives find and grep commands that search a hardcoded local Obsidian vault path. This is legitimate for the skill, but it can expose private notes if used without user consent.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The commands are explicit and target a local note vault. The risk is privacy exposure, not command injection, because the examples do not interpolate untrusted input.

low_findings.title: Weak Cryptography Static Finding Is False Positive
low_findings.description: The static analyzer flagged SKILL.md:3 as weak cryptography, but that line is the natural language description. No cryptographic algorithm appears there.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The line contains only descriptive text about Obsidian note management. There is no hash, cipher, encryption call, or crypto configuration.

low_findings.title: Markdown Backticks Misclassified As Execution
low_findings.description: Several external command findings are inline Markdown or fenced examples, not Ruby backtick execution. The actual commands are limited to find and grep examples.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited lines use Markdown code formatting for paths, note names, and wikilinks. They are not dynamic execution constructs.

dangerous_patterns.title: Recursive Local File Search
dangerous_patterns.description: The skill recommends recursive find and grep commands over the vault path. This can reveal sensitive personal note contents during normal operation.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The filesystem search behavior is directly documented. It is expected for this skill, but should be disclosed because Obsidian vaults can contain private data.

content.value_statement: Personal note vaults become hard to search and connect as they grow. This skill helps Claude, Codex, and Claude Code find notes, create notes, and maintain wikilink structure.
actual_capabilities: Search Markdown note filenames in a configured Obsidian vault.
Search note contents for a keyword or phrase.
Create new notes using title case naming.
Add Obsidian wikilinks to related notes.
Find backlinks by searching for a specific wikilink.
Find index notes by matching names that contain Index.
limitations: The vault path is hardcoded and may not match every user's system.
The skill does not include an Obsidian plugin or API integration.
Search examples depend on local shell tools such as find and grep.
It does not validate note quality or resolve broken links automatically.

use_cases.title: Find Existing Research Notes
use_cases.description: Search filenames and note contents before creating a new note.

use_cases.title: Create Connected Learning Notes
use_cases.description: Draft a new note with title case naming and related wikilinks.

use_cases.title: Maintain Topic Indexes
use_cases.description: Locate index notes and add links that group related topics.

prompt_templates.title: Search For A Note
prompt_templates.prompt: Search my Obsidian vault for notes about [topic]. Show matching filenames and likely related notes.

prompt_templates.title: Create A Linked Note
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a title case Obsidian note about [topic]. Add relevant wikilinks at the bottom.

prompt_templates.title: Find Backlinks
prompt_templates.prompt: Find notes that link to [[Note Title]]. Summarize the relationship between those notes.

prompt_templates.title: Update An Index
prompt_templates.prompt: Find the best index note for [topic]. Suggest new wikilinks to add and explain why each belongs.

output_examples.input: Search for notes about retrieval augmented generation.
output: Found matching notes by filename and content.
Identified the most relevant index note.
Suggested related wikilinks to review next.

output_examples.input: Create a note about prompt caching.
output: Created a title case note name.
Added related wikilinks at the bottom.

output_examples.input: Find backlinks for an existing note.
output: Searched for the wikilink across the vault.
Grouped linked notes by likely topic.

best_practices: Confirm the vault path before searching or creating notes.
Search for existing notes before adding a new note.
Ask before reading broad note content that may contain private information.
anti_patterns: Do not run broad searches without a clear user request.
Do not create duplicate notes when a related note already exists.
Do not add unrelated wikilinks only to increase link count.

faq.question: Does this skill require Obsidian to be open?
faq.answer: No. It works with Markdown files in the vault path.

faq.question: Can I use a different vault path?
faq.answer: Yes, but the skill instructions need to be adjusted for that path.

faq.question: Does it use the Obsidian API?
faq.answer: No. It uses local file search patterns and Markdown conventions.

faq.question: Can it create folders?
faq.answer: The skill prefers a flat root structure and uses links for organization.

faq.question: Is it safe for private notes?
faq.answer: Use caution. The skill can search local note contents, so confirm scope first.

faq.question: Which tools can use this skill?
faq.answer: The report lists support for Claude, Codex, and Claude Code.
